name: CSRF — Predictable Token Forgery
educational_objective: Understand that a CSRF token is only effective if it is cryptographically unpredictable. A token generated as MD5(userId + date) can be forged by any attacker who knows the userId (often public) and the current date.
scenario: ProfileHub — the CSRF token for /profile/update is generated server-side as MD5(userId + YYYY-MM-DD). The userId is visible in the public profile URL. An attacker who knows the formula and userId can generate a valid token without ever reading the victim's DOM.
ar_scenario: ProfileHub تُولّد CSRF token بالمعادلة MD5(userId + date). userId ظاهر في رابط الملف الشخصي — المهاجم يستطيع تزوير Token صالح.

hints_metadata.content: A CSRF token protects you only if the attacker cannot predict it. What information is used to generate this token? Is any of it public or guessable?
hints_metadata.ar_content: CSRF token يحميك فقط إذا كان المهاجم لا يستطيع تنبّؤه. ما المعلومات المستخدمة لتوليده؟ هل أيٌّ منها عام؟

hints_metadata.content: The token formula is MD5(userId + YYYY-MM-DD). The userId appears in the profile URL. Today's date is known. You have all the inputs needed to compute a valid token independently.
hints_metadata.ar_content: معادلة الـ token هي MD5(userId + YYYY-MM-DD). userId ظاهر في الرابط. التاريخ معروف. لديك كل المدخلات.

hints_metadata.content: Click 'Forge Token + Submit' — the UI computes MD5(userId + today) client-side and submits it to /profile/update. The server validates it as legitimate. Token forged. CSRF executed.
hints_metadata.ar_content: اضغط 'Forge Token + Submit' — الـ UI يحسب MD5(userId + today) ويُرسله لـ /profile/update. السيرفر يقبله. Token مزوَّر. CSRF مُنفَّذ.

flag_validation.note: Returned from /profile/update when forged predictable token passes server validation

hidden_solution_path.exploit_path: Read userId from profile URL → compute MD5(userId + YYYY-MM-DD) → POST /profile/update with forged token → server validates token → CSRF succeeds
hidden_solution_path.key_insight: Predictable token = no token. CSRF token must use CSPRNG, be session-bound, and single-use.
hidden_solution_path.fix_summary: Use crypto.randomBytes(32) or equivalent CSPRNG; bind token to session ID; rotate on each use
